subfamily_demarcation.description: Subfamily demarcation based on DEmARC analysis: PUD 46.8-51.0% (PPD 1.472-1.757) in concatenated aa sequences of 5 replicase domains.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation based on DEmARC analysis: PUD 35.1-36.0% (PPD 0.873-0.909) in concatenated aa sequences of 5 replicase domains.

subgenus_demarcation.description: Subgenus demarcation based on DEmARC analysis: PUD 13.2-14.2% (PPD 0.200-0.221) in concatenated aa sequences of 5 replicase domains.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation based on DEmARC analysis: PUD (percent uncorrected distance) 7.5% (PPD 0.095) in concatenated aa sequences of 5 replicase domains (3CLpro+NiRAN+RdRp+ZBD+HEL1).

coronaviridae.notes: MSL40: 6 genera, 28 subgenera, 54 species. DEmARC PUD thresholds (Table 4): species 7.5%, subgenus 13.2-14.2%, genus 35.1-36.0%, subfamily 46.8-51.0%. All based on concatenated 3CLpro+NiRAN+RdRp+ZBD+HEL1 aa sequences.

genus_demarcation.description: Members of all known picornavirus genera differ by: significant divergence exceeding 66% (per site) in P1 capsid and 64% in 2C helicase, 3C protease, and 3D polymerase. Also lack detectable homology in L, 2B, 3A, 3B proteins.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation by genus-specific aa divergence thresholds in polyprotein (<30%), P1 (30-40%), and 2C+3CD (20-30%). See genus_criteria.json for per-genus values.

picornaviridae.notes: Genus-specific species thresholds. Enterovirus: <30%/40%/30%; Hepatovirus: <30%/30%/30%; Aphthovirus: <30%/40%/20%; etc. See genus_criteria.json.

subfamily_demarcation.description: Subfamily demarcation based on genetic distance (substitutions per site) from node distinguishing Paramyxoviridae from Sunviridae, computed from complete L protein amino acid sequences.

genus_demarcation.description: Current taxonomic structure based on comparison of complete L protein amino acid sequences. Genus membership based on monophyly of L protein.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation entirely based on L protein aa phylogenetic tree branch length threshold of 0.03 (JTT matrix-based model). Uniform across all genera.

paramyxoviridae.notes: L protein is the sole molecular marker used for all levels. Branch length 0.03 for species (all genera). Genetic distances in substitutions per site.

genus_demarcation.description: Genera distinguished by phylogenetic analysis of conserved domains of NS5 (flaviviruses) or NS5B (hepaciviruses/pestiviruses) RdRp.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation varies by genus. Hepacivirus: >0.25 aa p-distance in NS3, >0.3 in NS5B. Pegivirus: >0.31 in NS3, >0.31-0.36 in NS5B. Orthoflavivirus: no numerical cutoff (ecology+phylogeny). See genus_criteria.json.

flaviviridae.notes: Genus-specific thresholds. Hepacivirus and Pegivirus have clear aa p-distance cutoffs. Orthoflavivirus uses ecology+phylogeny without numbers.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by deep phylogenetic divergence across whole coding region.

species_demarcation.description: Members of most species show at least 10% difference in amino acid identity over entire coding regions. Also considers antigenic characteristics, vector/host association, disease association, and ecological characteristics.

togaviridae.notes: Threshold is fluid: VEEV subtypes with 85% aa identity still considered same species due to biological relatedness. Everglades/Tonate/Mucambo are separate species at 94-97% identity due to ecological differences.

genus_demarcation.description: Members of different genera have >60% amino acid sequence difference in the complete VP1 (major capsid protein).

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation based on VP1 phylogeny and sequence divergence. No single fixed threshold stated.

caliciviridae.notes: Genus-level divergence matrix: Bavovirus-Lagovirus 81.5%, Bavovirus-Norovirus 85.2%, Lagovirus-Norovirus 81.4%.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation based on phylogeny of L protein and overall genome organisation.

species_demarcation.description: Genus-specific thresholds. Lyssavirus: 78-80% N gene nt identity. Vesiculovirus: 20% L / 10% N / 15% G aa divergence. Ephemerovirus: 15% L / 8% N. Novirhabdovirus: 31% G nt divergence. See genus_criteria.json.

rhabdoviridae.notes: Very diverse family (>40 genera). Per-genus thresholds in genus_criteria.json.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation based on phylogenetic distance, genome organisation, and host range.

species_demarcation.description: Species designation requires at least two of: phylogenetic distance >10-15% (ML analysis of pol aa), genome organization, host range, nucleotide composition, cross-neutralization.

adenoviridae.notes: DNA virus. Pol aa distance 10-15% threshold. If >15% + lack of cross-neutralization = different species. See genus_criteria.json for details.

subfamily_demarcation.description: Subfamily demarcation by DEmARC analysis: PED (pairwise evolutionary distance) threshold 3.5 on concatenated S+M+L amino acid sequences.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by DEmARC analysis: PED threshold 0.95 on concatenated S+M+L amino acid sequences.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation by DEmARC analysis: PED threshold 0.1 on concatenated S+M+L amino acid sequences. Also considers host association and geographic distribution.

hantaviridae.notes: Segmented negative-sense RNA genome (L, M, S). DEmARC PED thresholds: species 0.1, genus 0.95, subfamily 3.5. Host association is also important.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by PASC analysis: genomic sequences of members of different genera differ by >=55% (coding-complete genomes).

species_demarcation.description: Orthoebolavirus/Thamnovirus species: PASC genomic difference >=23%. Most genera are monotypic. Also considers gene overlaps, host/geographic distribution, pathogenicity.

filoviridae.notes: Orthoebolavirus >=23% genome difference for species. Most genera (7/9) are monotypic.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by phylogenetic analysis of conserved herpesvirus genes.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation based on phylogenetic clustering, host range, pathogenesis, and tissue tropism.

orthoherpesviridae.notes: Large dsDNA virus family. Formerly 'Herpesviridae'. Classification relies heavily on biology alongside molecular data.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation: members of the same genus share >40% S-segment nt identity AND >35% L-segment nt identity.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation: <80% nt identity in S RNA segment AND <76% in L RNA segment; association with distinct main host. Applies to Mammarenavirus, Hartmanivirus, Reptarenavirus, Antennavirus.

arenaviridae.notes: Segmented ambisense RNA (L, S). Species: S <80% + L <76% nt. Genus: S >40% + L >35% nt.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by phylogenetic analysis of Large T antigen.

species_demarcation.description: Polyomaviruses with >81% whole-genome nucleotide identity are considered the same species.

polyomaviridae.notes: DNA virus. Uses whole-genome nt identity for species demarcation.

subfamily_demarcation.description: L1 ORF of members of different subfamilies shares less than 45% sequence identity.

genus_demarcation.description: Originally: genus contains papillomaviruses with <60% nt identity in L1 ORF to members of other genera.

species_demarcation.description: A papillomavirus type (species) shares <90% nt identity in L1 ORF with any other type.

papillomaviridae.notes: DNA virus. Well-defined numerical thresholds at all levels based on L1 ORF.

amnoonviridae.notes: Monotypic family (single genus, single species). RdRp domain homologous to influenza C PB1.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation based on phylogenetic analysis of ORF1 amino acid sequences.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation based on <69% pairwise nucleotide identity in ORF1 gene sequences.

anelloviridae.notes: Circular ssDNA virus. ORF1 encodes capsid protein, used as primary classification marker.

subfamily_demarcation.description: Subfamily demarcation by DEmARC: PUD 39.7-49.7% (PPD 0.952-1.502) in concatenated aa sequences of 5 replicase domains.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by DEmARC: PUD 29.0-31.0% (PPD 0.578-0.637) in concatenated aa sequences of 5 replicase domains.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation by DEmARC: PUD 10.7-12.0% (PPD 0.174-0.196) in concatenated aa sequences of 5 replicase domains.

arteriviridae.notes: Uses DEmARC software for distance-based hierarchical demarcation across all ranks. 6 subfamilies, 13 genera, 23 species. Also has subgenus PUD 16.5-20.3%.

asfarviridae.notes: Interim ICTV report. No explicit demarcation criteria provided. Currently contains African swine fever virus and related viruses. Large dsDNA virus.

astroviridae.notes: Interim ICTV report. No explicit demarcation criteria provided in available text.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation based on ~45% complete genome nucleotide identity cut-off (PASC analysis) and differences in genome organisation (gene order variations in G and M genes).

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation by PASC of coding-complete genome sequences with cut-off at 72-75% nt identity. Plus antigenic relationships and host range.

bornaviridae.notes: PASC 72-75% nt identity for species. Gene order differs: Orthobornavirus (3'-N-X/P-G-M-L-5') vs Carbovirus (3'-N-X/P-M-G-L-5').

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation based on origin of replication (ori) location: Circovirus has ori on Rep-encoding strand, Cyclovirus has ori on Cp-encoding strand.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation based on 80% genome-wide nucleotide sequence identity threshold.

circoviridae.notes: Small circular ssDNA virus. Genus distinguished by genome organisation (ori position), species by 80% genome nt identity.

subfamily_demarcation.description: Subfamily demarcation based on host range, phylogeny, inter-subfamily amino acid p-distances >0.6 vs intra-subfamily <0.5, and genome organisation differences.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by phylogenetic analysis of ORF1 polyprotein residues 1-450 (methyltransferase domain).

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation by phylogenetic analysis of ORF1 methyltransferase domain alignment.

hepeviridae.notes: Subfamily threshold: inter >0.6 aa p-distance, intra <0.5. ORF1 methyltransferase domain (residues 1-450) used for genus/species.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by phylogenetic analysis of the L segment RdRp.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation based on <93% amino acid identity in RdRP sequence. Also considers vector association and geographic distribution.

nairoviridae.notes: Segmented negative-sense RNA genome (L, M, S). Tick-borne bunyaviruses. Species: <93% RdRP aa identity.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by host range, vectors, transmission mode, virion sedimentation coefficient, CP electrophoretic mobilities, antigenic properties, and RNA1/RNA2 phylogeny.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation based on <80% nucleotide identity and <87% amino acid identity in RNA2 segment.

nodaviridae.notes: Bipartite ssRNA(+) genome. RNA2 identity thresholds for species: <80% nt, <87% aa.

subfamily_demarcation.description: Subfamily demarcation based on host range (vertebrates vs invertebrates) and deep phylogenetic divergence in NS1 helicase domain.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by phylogenetic analysis of NS1 helicase domain and genome organisation differences.

species_demarcation.description: Viruses within a species are monophyletic and encode NS1/Rep proteins with >85% aa sequence identity. Applies to Bocaparvovirus, Dependoparvovirus, Erythroparvovirus, Protoparvovirus.

parvoviridae.notes: Small ssDNA virus. NS1/Rep >85% aa identity = same species. See genus_criteria.json.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation based on L protein monophyletic clade, NSs/NSm gene presence, and Gc protein N-terminal truncation patterns.

species_demarcation.description: Genus-specific species thresholds. Orthobunyavirus: <96% L protein aa identity. Gryffinivirus/Herbevirus: <90%. Pacuvirus: WAG distance >0.1. See genus_criteria.json.

peribunyaviridae.notes: Genus-specific L protein thresholds. Segmented negative-sense RNA genome (L, M, S).

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by phylogenetic analysis of L segment RdRp. 23 genera, 159 species.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation based on <95% amino acid identity in RdRP sequence. Also considers vector/host association and ecology.

phenuiviridae.notes: Very diverse family (23 genera). Segmented genome. Species: <95% RdRP aa identity. Ecology also considered.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation based on host specificity and strong sequence divergence of capsid proteins. Three genogroups with high sequence divergence (29-81% within, 18-25% between).

picobirnaviridae.notes: Bipartite dsRNA genome. Genogroup divergence: 29-81% identity within genogroups, 18-25% between. Classification still evolving.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by gene number (10 for Orthopneumovirus vs 8 for Metapneumovirus), NS1/NS2 gene presence, gene order (SH-G-F-M2 vs F-M2-SH-G), and genome length (15,191-15,277 nt vs 13,280-13,406 nt).

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation by phylogenetic analysis of L protein (RdRp) and overall sequence relatedness.

pneumoviridae.notes: Genera distinguished by gene count, gene order, genome length, and NS gene presence/absence.

subfamily_demarcation.description: Subfamily demarcation by host specificity (Chordopoxvirinae: vertebrates; Entomopoxvirinae: insects). Only 49 conserved gene products between subfamilies. 25 conserved genes used for ML phylogeny.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by phylogeny of 25 conserved genes, host range, and gene content/synteny.

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation based on phylogeny of conserved genes, host range specificity, and pathogenesis.

poxviridae.notes: Large dsDNA virus. Uses 25 conserved genes (A18, A2, A22, A23, A24, A28, A3, A32, A7, D1, D11, D12, D5, D6, E1, E10, E9, G5, H2, H4, H6, I8, J3, J6, L1) for family-level ML reconstruction.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by genome segment number (10, 11, or 12), host/vector range, capsid structure, and RdRp sequence divergence (<26% aa identity between genera, >30% within).

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation by segment reassortment capacity, nucleotide/amino acid sequence similarity, serological comparisons, electropherotype (AGE), and conserved terminal regions.

sedoreoviridae.notes: Segmented dsRNA genome (10-12 segments). Genus threshold: <26% RdRp aa identity between genera, >30% within genus.

genus_demarcation.description: Genus demarcation by genome segment number (9, 10, 11, or 12), host/vector range, capsid structure, and RdRp sequence divergence (<26% aa identity between genera, >30% within).

species_demarcation.description: Species demarcation by segment reassortment capacity, nucleotide/amino acid sequence similarity, serological comparisons, electropherotype (AGE), and conserved terminal regions.

spinareoviridae.notes: Segmented dsRNA genome (9-12 segments). Genus threshold: <26% RdRp aa identity between genera, >30% within genus. Distinguished from Sedoreoviridae by turreted capsid.
